godot_contract_refs.section: Deterministic spec compiler, cross-reference closure, runtime metadata, and canonical hashing
P2 spec compiler and byte-identical manifest completion gate

known_ambiguities: The clean EventFactor and Section sources are structural evidence for event-handler dispatch and typed resolver flow only; they do not define the Godot compiler fields, ordering, hashes, or maturity policy.

P2C proves strict authoring and global cross-reference closure and therefore promotes at most SPECIFIED; implementation bindings, executed tests, evidence freshness, coverage, and release promotion remain later P2 work.
EXPLICIT ruleset references fail closed until a versioned ruleset provider exists; ACTIVE feature IDs can be gated structurally, while feature implementation is deferred to its owning phase.
The tracked five-set authoring input remains empty, so committed source contains schemas and compiler logic but no proprietary or generated battle data.

Mechanism test requirements close static manifest coverage by exact kind, declared ACTIVE branch, distinct test ID, maturity threshold, and oracle superset; executed/passed evidence remains deferred and computed maturity is independently capped.
Resolver-level mechanism ownership may include indirect associations; scheduled phase mechanism_ids remain a validated subset rather than an exact derived union.

spec_manifest.json and runtime_manifest.json are reproducible local outputs under the ignored battle/generated/battle_specs boundary or the system temp directory, use bounded no-follow immutable publication, and are never committed as source truth.
The runtime catalog intentionally excludes authoring paths, debug and owner labels, evidence, project requirements, tests, target/computed maturity, timestamps, GUIDs, and runtime object instances.
